summary.intent: Modernize the Moltis website to better showcase supported AI providers/LLMs (like Polyphony's pills UI), add missing feature context, and improve positioning relative to competitors.
summary.outcome: Added a 'Works with your stack' pills section with 23 brand icons, extracted CSS to style.css, rewrote hero title/subtitle, added positioning paragraph, 3-step 'How it works' section, use-cases grid, community quote, and committed/pushed to PR #567.

repo: Website lives in website/ directory under the moltis worktree at /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/thoughtful-pint/website/
Only index.en.html was modified — other locale files exist and need separate propagation
CSS was originally inlined in index.en.html; now extracted to website/style.css
Icons live in website/images/icons/ — mix of SVG (official brand icons) and raster (.webp, .png) for LM Studio and MiniMax
Moltis uses an orange/red accent color theme (not purple like Polyphony)
Dark mode is handled via a .dark CSS class, not prefers-color-scheme media query
Simple Icons (simpleicons.org) is the canonical source for official brand SVGs

code.finding: pill-icon class uses filter:invert(1) for dark mode on SVGs; pill-icon-color class skips invert and uses grayscale(1) for raster logos like LM Studio and MiniMax
Pills section uses scroll-reveal JS (IntersectionObserver) for fade-up animation on each pill-group row
MiniMax PNG was a full wordmark; had to crop to left ~30% to get just the waveform icon using ImageMagick

workflow: Simple Icons CDN (cdn.simpleicons.org) provides clean brand SVGs ready to use
For brand SVGs used as tags, currentColor doesn't work — CSS filter:invert is the dark mode strategy instead
Raster logos (webp/png) need grayscale filter to match monochrome SVG icons visually
Playwright MCP was used for visual preview/screenshot of the dev server during development
Dev server was started as background task and killed after preview

friction: Several inline SVGs were initially hand-crafted and looked broken at small sizes (Teams 'T' glyph, DeepSeek heart) — required multiple rounds of replacement
Cerebras SVG had a white background that needed to be removed for dark mode compatibility
MiniMax logo included full wordmark text requiring image cropping rather than a simple download
LM Studio has no official SVG — fell back to raster webp from their website
User hit a Claude usage limit mid-session and had to wait/resume
Multiple background dev server task notifications cluttered the conversation flow
The Z.AI icon had to be manually crafted as a lettermark since no official SVG was available
open_items: Changes only applied to index.en.html — other locale files need to be updated separately
Social proof section is thin: only one Discord quote (Noita); more testimonials/community quotes should be added when available
LM Studio icon is a raster .webp — would be better replaced with an official SVG if one becomes available
MiniMax, Z.AI, and Venice pills have no icon or custom lettermarks — official icons may become available
Screenshot gallery was not integrated into the 'How it works' flow as suggested in the analysis
PR #567 created but no review/merge confirmed
